I've uploaded a code repository in the directory . Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
MudDataGrid: Add event for AFTER CommittedItemChanges
### Describe your idea

When `EditMode` is `DataGridEditMode.Form` and `CommittedItemChanges` fires, the entity passed to the event handler is a copy of the bound item, and the edits are not applied to the bound item until after the event handler returns.

In my use case, I have an object with a collection property bound to the grid. When the object is edited in my UI, a copy of the original object is made, and after any property of the object changes, the current object is checked for equality against the original copy to determine if the object has changed (in order to enable a save button for example). When an item in the object's collection property is edited via `DataGridEditMode.Form`, the only way to know when the editing is complete is via the `CommittedItemChanges` event, but because the edits have not yet been applied, comparing the original object to the current object results in no changes even when an item in the data grid has been changed.

There should be a final event such as `CommittedItemChanged` that fires after changes have been applied so there any equality checks on objects that reference the edited item will operate on the actual changed object and not a copy of the object.

### Example code and screenshots

_No response_

</pr_description>

    65	            Selection = new HashSet<T>(Comparer);
    66	            SelectedItems = new HashSet<T>(Comparer);
    67	            using var registerScope = CreateRegisterScope();
    68	            registerScope.RegisterParameter<IEnumerable<T>?>(nameof(Items))
    69	                .WithParameter(() => Items)
    70	                .WithChangeHandler(OnItemsChangedAsync);
    71	
    72	            _selectedItemState = registerScope.RegisterParameter<T?>(nameof(SelectedItem))
    73	                .WithParameter(() => SelectedItem)
    74	                .WithEventCallback(() => SelectedItemChanged)
    75	                .WithChangeHandler(OnSelectedItemChangedAsync);
    76

    78	                .WithParameter(() => SelectedItems)

    81

    83	                .WithParameter(() => ExpandSingleRow)
    84	                .WithChangeHandler(OnExpandSingleRowChangedAsync);
    85	        }

OBSERVATION:

        /// Occurs when a row has been right-clicked.
        /// </summary>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<DataGridRowClickEventArgs<T>> RowContextMenuClick { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when edit mode begins for an item.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// If changes are committed, the <see cref="CommittedItemChanges"/> delegate is called. If editing is canceled, the <see cref="CanceledEditingItem"/> occurs.
        /// </remarks>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<T> StartedEditingItem { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when editing of an item has been canceled.
        /// </summary>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<T> CanceledEditingItem { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Invoked when the user saves changes to an item, allowing for validation, 
        /// persistence, or other processing.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// <para>
        /// The callback receives a separate instance of the item with the committed values, 
        /// preventing changes from being applied to the underlying data source before 
        /// validation or processing completes.
        /// </para>
        /// <para>
        /// Return a <see cref="DataGridEditFormAction"/> value to control the edit form behavior. 
        /// When <see cref="EditMode"/> is <see cref="DataGridEditMode.Form"/>:
        /// </para>
        /// <list type="bullet">
        /// <item><see cref="DataGridEditFormAction.Close"/> - Accepts changes and closes the form</item>
        /// <item><see cref="DataGridEditFormAction.KeepOpen"/> - Rejects changes and keeps the form open for corrections</item>
        /// </list>
        /// </remarks>
        [Parameter]
        public Func<T, Task<DataGridEditFormAction>>? CommittedItemChanges { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when a field changes in the edit dialog.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// This event only occurs when <see cref="EditMode"/> is <see cref="DataGridEditMode.Form"/>.
        /// </remarks>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<FormFieldChangedEventArgs> FormFieldChanged { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when hierarchy visibility toggled.
        /// </summary>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<DataGridHierarchyVisibilityToggledEventArgs<T>> HierarchyVisibilityToggled { get; set; }

        #endregion

        #region Parameters

        internal void ClearEditingItem()
        {
            _editingItem = default;
            _editingSourceItem = default;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// This method notifies the consumer that changes to the data have been committed
        /// and what those changes are. This variation of the method is only used by the Cell
        /// when the EditMode is set to cell.
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="item"></param>
        /// <returns></returns>
        internal async Task CommitItemChangesAsync(T item)
        {
            // Here, we need to validate at the cellular level...
            if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
                _ = await CommittedItemChanges(item); // ignore return value in cell edit mode
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// This method notifies the consumer that changes to the data have been committed
        /// and what those changes are. This variation of the method is used when the EditMode
        /// is anything but Cell since the _editingItem is used.
        /// </summary>
        /// <returns></returns>
        internal async Task CommitItemChangesAsync()
        {
            Debug.Assert(_editingItem is not null);
            Debug.Assert(_editForm is not null);
            await _editForm.ValidateAsync();
            if (!_editForm.IsValid)
            {
                return;
            }

            if (_editingSourceItem != null)
            {
                if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
                {
                    var closeBehavior = await CommittedItemChanges(_editingItem);

                    if (closeBehavior == DataGridEditFormAction.KeepOpen)
                        return;
                }

                foreach (var property in _properties.Where(p => p.CanWrite))
                    property.SetValue(_editingSourceItem, property.GetValue(_editingItem));

                ClearEditingItem();
                _isEditFormOpen = false;
            }
        }

        internal async Task OnRowClickedAsync(MouseEventArgs args, T item, int rowIndex)
        {
            await RowClick.InvokeAsync(new DataGridRowClickEventArgs<T>(args, item, rowIndex));

            if (EditMode != DataGridEditMode.Cell && EditTrigger == DataGridEditTrigger.OnRowClick)
                await SetEditingItemAsync(item);

            await SetSelectedItemAsync(item);
        }

        internal Task OnContextMenuClickedAsync(MouseEventArgs args, T item, int rowIndex)
        {
            return RowContextMenuClick.InvokeAsync(new DataGridRowClickEventArgs<T>(args, item, rowIndex));
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Gets the total count of filtered items in the data grid.
        /// </summary>
        /// <returns>
        /// The number of items remaining after applying filters.  When <see cref="ServerData"/> is in use, the <see cref="GridData{T}.TotalItems"/> value is returned.
        /// </returns>
        public int GetFilteredItemsCount()
        {
            if (HasServerData)
                return _serverData.TotalItems;
            return FilteredItems.Count();
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Navigates to a page when this grid has a <see cref="MudDataGridPager{T}"/>.
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="page">The page to navigate to.</param>
        public void NavigateTo(Page page)
        {
            CurrentPage = page switch
            {
                Page.First => 0,
                Page.Last => Math.Max(0, numPages - 1),
                Page.Next => Math.Min(numPages - 1, CurrentPage + 1),
                Page.Previous => Math.Max(0, CurrentPage - 1),
                _ => CurrentPage
            };

            GroupItems();
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Invoked when the user saves changes to an item, allowing for validation, 
        /// persistence, or other processing.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// <para>
        /// The callback receives a separate instance of the item with the committed values, 
        /// preventing changes from being applied to the underlying data source before 
        /// validation or processing completes.
        /// </para>
        /// <para>
        /// Return a <see cref="DataGridEditFormAction"/> value to control the edit form behavior. 
        /// When <see cref="EditMode"/> is <see cref="DataGridEditMode.Form"/>:
        /// </para>
        /// <list type="bullet">
        /// <item><see cref="DataGridEditFormAction.Close"/> - Accepts changes and closes the form</item>
        /// <item><see cref="DataGridEditFormAction.KeepOpen"/> - Rejects changes and keeps the form open for corrections</item>
        /// </list>
        /// </remarks>
        [Parameter]
        public Func<T, Task<DataGridEditFormAction>>? CommittedItemChanges { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when a field changes in the edit dialog.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// This event only occurs when <see cref="EditMode"/> is <see cref="DataGridEditMode.Form"/>.
        /// </remarks>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<FormFieldChangedEventArgs> FormFieldChanged { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// This method notifies the consumer that changes to the data have been committed
        /// and what those changes are. This variation of the method is only used by the Cell
        /// when the EditMode is set to cell.
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="item"></param>
        /// <returns></returns>
        internal async Task CommitItemChangesAsync(T item)
        {
            // Here, we need to validate at the cellular level...
            if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
                _ = await CommittedItemChanges(item); // ignore return value in cell edit mode
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// This method notifies the consumer that changes to the data have been committed
        /// and what those changes are. This variation of the method is used when the EditMode
        /// is anything but Cell since the _editingItem is used.
        /// </summary>
        /// <returns></returns>
        internal async Task CommitItemChangesAsync()
        {
            Debug.Assert(_editingItem is not null);
            Debug.Assert(_editForm is not null);
            await _editForm.ValidateAsync();
            if (!_editForm.IsValid)
            {
                return;
            }

            if (_editingSourceItem != null)
            {
                if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
                {
                    var closeBehavior = await CommittedItemChanges(_editingItem);

                    if (closeBehavior == DataGridEditFormAction.KeepOpen)
                        return;
                }

                foreach (var property in _properties.Where(p => p.CanWrite))
                    property.SetValue(_editingSourceItem, property.GetValue(_editingItem));

                ClearEditingItem();
                _isEditFormOpen = false;
            }
        }

        internal async Task OnRowClickedAsync(MouseEventArgs args, T item, int rowIndex)

Now I understand the issue clearly:

1. In `CommitItemChangesAsync()` (the form version), `CommittedItemChanges` is called BEFORE changes are applied to the source item.
2. The PR requests a new event `CommittedItemChanged` that fires AFTER changes have been applied.

Let me also look at the first CommitItemChangesAsync(T item) for the cell mode - it seems like in cell mode, the item passed is already the source item (modified directly), so the "after" event should also fire there.

Now let me write a script to reproduce the issue and then implement the fix.

        [Test]
        public async Task DataGridEventCallbacks()
        {
            var comp = Context.Render<DataGridEventCallbacksTest>();
            var dataGrid = comp.FindComponent<MudDataGrid<DataGridEventCallbacksTest.Item>>();

            // Include callbacks in test coverage.
            dataGrid.Instance.RowClick.HasDelegate.Should().Be(true);
            dataGrid.Instance.RowContextMenuClick.HasDelegate.Should().Be(true);
            dataGrid.Instance.SelectedItemChanged.HasDelegate.Should().Be(true);
            dataGrid.Instance.CommittedItemChanges.Should().NotBeNull();
            dataGrid.Instance.StartedEditingItem.HasDelegate.Should().Be(true);
            dataGrid.Instance.CanceledEditingItem.HasDelegate.Should().Be(true);
            dataGrid.Instance.CanceledEditingItem.Should().Be(dataGrid.Instance.CanceledEditingItem);

            // we test to make sure that we can set and get the cancelCallback via the CancelledEditingItem property
            var cancelCallback = dataGrid.Instance.CanceledEditingItem;
            await dataGrid.SetParametersAndRenderAsync(parameters => parameters.Add(dg => dg.CanceledEditingItem, () => { }));
            dataGrid.Instance.CanceledEditingItem.Should().NotBe(cancelCallback);
#pragma warning disable BL0005
            dataGrid.Instance.CanceledEditingItem = cancelCallback;
#pragma warning restore BL0005
            dataGrid.Instance.CanceledEditingItem.Should().Be(cancelCallback);

            // Set some parameters manually so that they are covered.
            await dataGrid.SetParametersAndRenderAsync(parameters => parameters
                .Add(x => x.MultiSelection, true)
                .Add(x => x.ReadOnly, false)
                .Add(x => x.EditMode, DataGridEditMode.Cell)
                .Add(x => x.EditTrigger, DataGridEditTrigger.OnRowClick));

            // Make sure that the callbacks have not been fired yet.
            comp.Instance.RowClicked.Should().Be(false);
            comp.Instance.RowContextMenuClicked.Should().Be(false);
            comp.Instance.SelectedItemChanged.Should().Be(false);
            comp.Instance.CommittedItemChanges.Should().Be(false);
            comp.Instance.StartedEditingItem.Should().Be(false);
            comp.Instance.CanceledEditingItem.Should().Be(false);

            // Fire RowClick, SelectedItemChanged, SelectedItemsChanged, and StartedEditingItem callbacks.
            await dataGrid.FindAll(".mud-table-body tr")[0].ClickAsync();

            // Fire RowContextMenuClick
            dataGrid.FindAll(".mud-table-body tr")[0].ContextMenu();

            // Edit an item.
            await dataGrid.FindAll(".mud-table-body tr td input")[0].ChangeAsync("A test");

            // Make sure that the callbacks have been fired.
            comp.Instance.RowClicked.Should().Be(true);
            comp.Instance.RowContextMenuClicked.Should().Be(true);
            comp.Instance.SelectedItemChanged.Should().Be(true);
            comp.Instance.CommittedItemChanges.Should().Be(true);
            comp.Instance.CanceledEditingItem.Should().Be(false);

            // TODO: Triggering of the CancelEditingItem callback appears to require the Form edit mode
            // but we can brute force it by directly calling the CancelEditingItemAsync method on the datagrid
            await dataGrid.InvokeAsync(dataGrid.Instance.CancelEditingItemAsync);
            comp.Instance.CanceledEditingItem.Should().Be(true);
        }

﻿// Copyright (c) MudBlazor 2021
// MudBlazor licenses this file to you under the MIT license.
// See the LICENSE file in the project root for more information.

using System.Collections.Specialized;
using System.Diagnostics;
using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;
using System.Globalization;
using System.Reflection;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.Virtualization;
using MudBlazor.State;
using MudBlazor.Utilities;
using MudBlazor.Utilities.Clone;

namespace MudBlazor
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Represents a sortable, filterable data grid with multiselection, pagination, editing, grouping, aggregation, and server-side <see cref="IQueryable{T}"/> support.
    /// </summary>
    /// <typeparam name="T">The type of data represented by each row in this grid.</typeparam>
    [CascadingTypeParameter(nameof(T))]
    public partial class MudDataGrid<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicProperties)] T> : MudComponentBase, IDisposable
    {
        private MudForm? _editForm;
        internal int? _rowsPerPage;
        private int _currentPage = 0;
        internal bool _groupInitialExpanded = true;
        internal MudVirtualize<IndexBag<T>>? _mudVirtualize;
        private bool _isFirstRendered = false;
        private bool _filtersMenuVisible = false;
        private bool _columnsPanelVisible = false;
        internal HashSet<T> _openHierarchies = [];
        private readonly HashSet<T> _initialExpansions = [];
        private Func<T, bool>? _initialExpandedFunc = null;
        private Func<T, bool>? _buttonDisabledFunc = null;
        private string _columnsPanelSearch = string.Empty;
        private MudDropContainer<Column<T>>? _dropContainer;
        private MudDropContainer<Column<T>>? _columnsPanelDropContainer;
        private readonly PropertyInfo[] _properties = typeof(T).GetProperties();
        private CancellationTokenSource? _serverDataCancellationTokenSource;
        private IEnumerable<T>? _currentRenderFilteredItemsCache = null;
        internal GroupDefinition<T>? _groupDefinition;
        internal Dictionary<GroupKey, bool> _groupExpansionsDict = [];
        private GridData<T> _serverData = new() { TotalItems = 0, Items = Array.Empty<T>() };
        private Func<IFilterDefinition<T>> _defaultFilterDefinitionFactory = () => new FilterDefinition<T>();
        internal (double Top, double Left) _openPosition = (0, 0);

        private readonly ParameterState<T?> _selectedItemState;
        private readonly ParameterState<HashSet<T>?> _selectedItemsState;
        private readonly ParameterState<bool> _expandSingleRowState;

        /// <summary>
        /// Inline data attributes for positioning the menu at the cursor's location.
        /// </summary>
        internal Dictionary<string, object> PositionAttributes => new()
        {
            { "data-pc-x", _openPosition.Left.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture) },
            { "data-pc-y", _openPosition.Top.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture) }
        };

        public MudDataGrid()
        {
            Selection = new HashSet<T>(Comparer);
            SelectedItems = new HashSet<T>(Comparer);
            using var registerScope = CreateRegisterScope();
            registerScope.RegisterParameter<IEnumerable<T>?>(nameof(Items))
                .WithParameter(() => Items)
                .WithChangeHandler(OnItemsChangedAsync);

            _selectedItemState = registerScope.RegisterParameter<T?>(nameof(SelectedItem))
                .WithParameter(() => SelectedItem)
                .WithEventCallback(() => SelectedItemChanged)
                .WithChangeHandler(OnSelectedItemChangedAsync);

            _selectedItemsState = registerScope.RegisterParameter<HashSet<T>?>(nameof(SelectedItems))
                .WithParameter(() => SelectedItems)
                .WithEventCallback(() => SelectedItemsChanged)
                .WithChangeHandler(OnSelectedItemsChanged);

            _expandSingleRowState = registerScope.RegisterParameter<bool>(nameof(ExpandSingleRow))
                .WithParameter(() => ExpandSingleRow)
                .WithChangeHandler(OnExpandSingleRowChangedAsync);
        }

        protected string Classname =>
            new CssBuilder("mud-table")
                .AddClass("mud-data-grid")
                .AddClass("mud-xs-table", Breakpoint == Breakpoint.Xs)
                .AddClass("mud-sm-table", Breakpoint == Breakpoint.Sm)
                .AddClass("mud-md-table", Breakpoint == Breakpoint.Md)
                .AddClass("mud-lg-table", Breakpoint == Breakpoint.Lg || Breakpoint == Breakpoint.Always)
                .AddClass("mud-xl-table", Breakpoint == Breakpoint.Xl || Breakpoint == Breakpoint.Always)
                .AddClass("mud-table-dense", Dense)
                .AddClass("mud-table-hover", Hover)
                .AddClass("mud-table-bordered", Bordered)
                .AddClass("mud-table-striped", Striped)
                .AddClass("mud-table-outlined", Outlined)
                .AddClass("mud-table-square", Square)
                .AddClass("mud-table-sticky-header", FixedHeader)
                .AddClass("mud-table-sticky-footer", FixedFooter)
                .AddClass($"mud-elevation-{Elevation}", !Outlined)
                .AddClass(Class)
                .Build();

        protected string Stylename =>
            new StyleBuilder()
                .AddStyle("overflow-x", "auto", when: HorizontalScrollbar || ColumnResizeMode == ResizeMode.Container)
                .AddStyle("position", "relative", when: HasStickyColumns)
                .AddStyle(Style)
                .Build();

        protected string TableStyle =>
            new StyleBuilder()
                .AddStyle("height", Height, !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(Height))
                .AddStyle("width", "max-content", when: HorizontalScrollbar || ColumnResizeMode == ResizeMode.Container)
                .AddStyle("overflow", "clip", when: (HorizontalScrollbar || ColumnResizeMode == ResizeMode.Container) && HasStickyColumns)
                .AddStyle("display", "block", when: HorizontalScrollbar)
                .Build();

        protected string TableClass =>
            new CssBuilder("mud-table-container")
                .AddClass("cursor-col-resize", when: IsResizing)
                .Build();

        protected string HeadClassname =>
            new CssBuilder("mud-table-head")
                .AddClass(HeaderClass)
                .AddClass("mud-table-dense", Dense)
                .Build();

        protected string FootClassname =>
            new CssBuilder("mud-table-foot")
                .AddClass(FooterClass).Build();

        protected string BodyClassname =>
            new CssBuilder("mud-table-body")
                .AddClass("mud-table-body-virtualized", Virtualize)
                .Build();

        protected string HeaderFooterStyle =>
            new StyleBuilder()
                .AddStyle("position", "sticky", when: HasStickyColumns)
                .AddStyle("left", "0px", when: HasStickyColumns)
                .Build();

        protected override void OnParametersSet()
        {
            base.OnParametersSet();
            if (Items != null)
            {
                if (ServerData != null)
                {
                    throw new InvalidOperationException(
                        $"{GetType()} can only accept one item source from its parameters. " +
                        $"Do not supply both '{nameof(Items)}' and '{nameof(ServerData)}'.");
                }
                if (VirtualizeServerData != null)
                {
                    throw new InvalidOperationException(
                        $"{GetType()} can only accept one item source from its parameters. " +
                        $"Do not supply both '{nameof(Items)}' and '{nameof(VirtualizeServerData)}'.");
                }
                return;
            }

            if (VirtualizeServerData != null)
            {
                if (ServerData != null)
                {
                    throw new InvalidOperationException(
                        $"{GetType()} can only accept one item source from its parameters. " +
                        $"Do not supply both '{nameof(VirtualizeServerData)}' and '{nameof(ServerData)}'.");
                }
                if (QuickFilter != null)
                {
                    throw new InvalidOperationException(
                        $"Do not supply both '{nameof(VirtualizeServerData)}' and '{nameof(QuickFilter)}'.");
                }
                return;
            }

            if (ServerData != null && QuickFilter != null)
            {
                throw new InvalidOperationException(
                    $"Do not supply both '{nameof(ServerData)}' and '{nameof(QuickFilter)}'.");
            }
        }

        internal SortDirection GetColumnSortDirection(string? columnName)
        {
            if (columnName == null)
            {
                return SortDirection.None;
            }

            if (SortDefinitions.TryGetValue(columnName, out var sortDefinition))
            {
                return sortDefinition.Descending ? SortDirection.Descending : SortDirection.Ascending;
            }

            return SortDirection.None;
        }

        protected int numPages
        {
            get
            {
                if (HasServerData)
                    return (int)Math.Ceiling(_serverData.TotalItems / (double)RowsPerPage);

                return (int)Math.Ceiling(FilteredItems.Count() / (double)RowsPerPage);
            }
        }

        internal static bool RenderedColumnsItemsSelector(Column<T> item, string dropZone) => item?.PropertyName == dropZone;

        private static void Swap<TItem>(List<TItem> list, int indexA, int indexB)
        {
            (list[indexB], list[indexA]) = (list[indexA], list[indexB]);
        }

        private Task ItemUpdatedAsync(MudItemDropInfo<Column<T>> dropItem)
        {
            Debug.Assert(dropItem.Item is not null);
            dropItem.Item.Identifier = dropItem.DropzoneIdentifier;

            var dragAndDropSource = RenderedColumns.SingleOrDefault(rc => rc.PropertyName == dropItem.Item?.PropertyName);
            var dragAndDropDestination = RenderedColumns.SingleOrDefault(rc => rc.PropertyName == dropItem.DropzoneIdentifier);
            if (dragAndDropSource != null && dragAndDropDestination != null)
            {
                var dragAndDropSourceIndex = RenderedColumns.IndexOf(dragAndDropSource);
                var dragAndDropDestinationIndex = RenderedColumns.IndexOf(dragAndDropDestination);

                Swap(RenderedColumns, dragAndDropSourceIndex, dragAndDropDestinationIndex);

                Debug.Assert(dragAndDropSource.HeaderCell is not null);
                Debug.Assert(dragAndDropDestination.HeaderCell is not null);

                // swap source / destination
                var dest = dragAndDropDestination.HeaderCell.Width;
                var src = dragAndDropSource.HeaderCell.Width;

                dragAndDropSource.HeaderCell.Width = dest;
                dragAndDropDestination.HeaderCell.Width = src;

                StateHasChanged();
            }
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// The columns currently being displayed.
        /// </summary>
        public readonly List<Column<T>> RenderedColumns = new List<Column<T>>();

        internal T? _editingItem;

        internal T? _editingSourceItem;

        internal T? _previousEditingItem;
        internal bool _isEditFormOpen;

        // converters
        private readonly IConverter<bool, bool?> _oppositeBoolConverter = Conversions.From(value => !value, (bool? value) => !value ?? true);

        #region Notify Children Delegates

        internal Action<Dictionary<string, SortDefinition<T>>, HashSet<string>?>? SortChangedEvent { get; set; }
        internal Action<HashSet<T>>? SelectedItemsChangedEvent { get; set; }
        internal Action<bool>? SelectedAllItemsChangedEvent { get; set; }
        internal Action? StartedEditingItemEvent { get; set; }
        internal Action? EditingCanceledEvent { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when the pager state has changed.
        /// </summary>
        public Action? PagerStateHasChangedEvent { get; set; }

        #endregion

        #region EventCallbacks

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when the <see cref="SelectedItem"/> has changed.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// This typically occurs when a row has been clicked.
        /// </remarks>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<T?> SelectedItemChanged { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when the <see cref="SelectedItems"/> have changed.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// This typically occurs when one or more rows have been clicked when <see cref="MultiSelection"/> is <c>true</c>.
        /// </remarks>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<HashSet<T>?> SelectedItemsChanged { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when a row has been clicked.
        /// </summary>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<DataGridRowClickEventArgs<T>> RowClick { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when a row has been right-clicked.
        /// </summary>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<DataGridRowClickEventArgs<T>> RowContextMenuClick { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when edit mode begins for an item.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// If changes are committed, the <see cref="CommittedItemChanges"/> delegate is called. If editing is canceled, the <see cref="CanceledEditingItem"/> occurs.
        /// </remarks>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<T> StartedEditingItem { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when editing of an item has been canceled.
        /// </summary>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<T> CanceledEditingItem { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Invoked when the user saves changes to an item, allowing for validation, 
        /// persistence, or other processing.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// <para>
        /// The callback receives a separate instance of the item with the committed values, 
        /// preventing changes from being applied to the underlying data source before 
        /// validation or processing completes.
        /// </para>
        /// <para>
        /// Return a <see cref="DataGridEditFormAction"/> value to control the edit form behavior. 
        /// When <see cref="EditMode"/> is <see cref="DataGridEditMode.Form"/>:
        /// </para>
        /// <list type="bullet">
        /// <item><see cref="DataGridEditFormAction.Close"/> - Accepts changes and closes the form</item>
        /// <item><see cref="DataGridEditFormAction.KeepOpen"/> - Rejects changes and keeps the form open for corrections</item>
        /// </list>
        /// </remarks>
        [Parameter]
        public Func<T, Task<DataGridEditFormAction>>? CommittedItemChanges { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when a field changes in the edit dialog.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// This event only occurs when <see cref="EditMode"/> is <see cref="DataGridEditMode.Form"/>.
        /// </remarks>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<FormFieldChangedEventArgs> FormFieldChanged { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Occurs when hierarchy visibility toggled.
        /// </summary>
        [Parameter]
        public EventCallback<DataGridHierarchyVisibilityToggledEventArgs<T>> HierarchyVisibilityToggled { get; set; }

        internal async Task CommitItemChangesAsync(T item)
        {
            // Here, we need to validate at the cellular level...
            if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
                _ = await CommittedItemChanges(item); // ignore return value in cell edit mode
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// This method notifies the consumer that changes to the data have been committed
        /// and what those changes are. This variation of the method is used when the EditMode
        /// is anything but Cell since the _editingItem is used.
        /// </summary>
        /// <returns></returns>
        internal async Task CommitItemChangesAsync()
        {
            Debug.Assert(_editingItem is not null);
            Debug.Assert(_editForm is not null);
            await _editForm.ValidateAsync();
            if (!_editForm.IsValid)
            {
                return;
            }

            if (_editingSourceItem != null)
            {
                if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
                {
                    var closeBehavior = await CommittedItemChanges(_editingItem);

                    if (closeBehavior == DataGridEditFormAction.KeepOpen)
                        return;
                }

                foreach (var property in _properties.Where(p => p.CanWrite))
                    property.SetValue(_editingSourceItem, property.GetValue(_editingItem));

OBSERVATION:

        internal async Task CommitItemChangesAsync(T item)
        {
            // Here, we need to validate at the cellular level...
            if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
                _ = await CommittedItemChanges(item); // ignore return value in cell edit mode

            await CommittedItemChanged.InvokeAsync(item);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// This method notifies the consumer that changes to the data have been committed
        /// and what those changes are. This variation of the method is used when the EditMode
        /// is anything but Cell since the _editingItem is used.
        /// </summary>
        /// <returns></returns>
        internal async Task CommitItemChangesAsync()
        {
            Debug.Assert(_editingItem is not null);
            Debug.Assert(_editForm is not null);
            await _editForm.ValidateAsync();
            if (!_editForm.IsValid)
            {
                return;
            }

            if (_editingSourceItem != null)
            {
                if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
                {
                    var closeBehavior = await CommittedItemChanges(_editingItem);

                    if (closeBehavior == DataGridEditFormAction.KeepOpen)
                        return;
                }

                foreach (var property in _properties.Where(p => p.CanWrite))
                    property.SetValue(_editingSourceItem, property.GetValue(_editingItem));

                await CommittedItemChanged.InvokeAsync(_editingSourceItem);

                ClearEditingItem();
                _isEditFormOpen = false;
            }
        }

        internal async Task OnRowClickedAsync(MouseEventArgs args, T item, int rowIndex)

2026-05-29 12:51:26,047 - INFO - swea-agent-MudBlazor__MudBlazor-12907 - 💭 THOUGHT
That file references `CommittedItemChanges` in its doc comments. While it's not strictly necessary to update these comments, it might be good to keep them consistent. But the PR description doesn't mention changes to this file. Let me leave it as is.

Now let me think about edge cases:

1. **Cell edit mode**: In `CommitItemChangesAsync(T item)`, the item is already the modified source item. `CommittedItemChanges` is called first, then `CommittedItemChanged`. This is correct.

2. **Form edit mode**: In `CommitItemChangesAsync()`, the flow is:
   - Validate form
   - Call `CommittedItemChanges(_editingItem)` - this gets the copy
   - If `KeepOpen`, return early (and `CommittedItemChanged` is NOT called, which is correct)
   - Copy properties from `_editingItem` to `_editingSourceItem`
   - Call `CommittedItemChanged(_editingSourceItem)` - this gets the actual updated bound item
   - Clear editing state

3. **Validation fails**: If `_editForm.IsValid` is false, we return early before `CommittedItemChanges` or `CommittedItemChanged` are called. This is correct.

4. **`_editingSourceItem` is null**: The whole block is wrapped in `if (_editingSourceItem != null)`, so we won't accidentally invoke with null.

Let me also check if the `CommittedItemChanged` event should also be invoked when `CommittedItemChanges` returns `KeepOpen`. Based on the PR description, the user wants an event that fires AFTER changes have been applied. If `KeepOpen` is returned, changes are NOT applied (the form stays open), so we should NOT fire `CommittedItemChanged`. The current implementation handles this correctly because the `return` statement for `KeepOpen` happens before the changes are applied and before `CommittedItemChanged` is called.

Wait, let me double-check this logic. In the form version:

```
if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
{
    var closeBehavior = await CommittedItemChanges(_editingItem);
    if (closeBehavior == DataGridEditFormAction.KeepOpen)
        return;
}

foreach (var property in _properties.Where(p => p.CanWrite))
    property.SetValue(_editingSourceItem, property.GetValue(_editingItem));

await CommittedItemChanged.InvokeAsync(_editingSourceItem);
```

Yes, if `KeepOpen` is returned, we return before applying changes and before firing `CommittedItemChanged`. This is correct.

     9	namespace MudBlazor
    10	{
    11	    internal class Cell<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicProperties)] T>
    12	    {
    13	        private readonly MudDataGrid<T> _dataGrid;
    14	        private readonly Column<T> _column;
    15	        internal T _item;
    16	        internal string? _valueString;
    17	        internal double? _valueNumber;
    18	        internal bool _editing;
    19	        internal CellContext<T> _cellContext;
    20	
    21	        #region Computed Properties
    22	
    23	        internal object? ComputedValue
    24	        {
    25	            get
    26	            {
    27	                return _column.CellContent(_item);
    28	            }
    29	        }
    30	
    31	        internal string ComputedClass =>
    32	            new CssBuilder("mud-table-cell")
    33	                .AddClass("mud-table-cell-hide", _column.HideSmall)
    34	                .AddClass("sticky-left", _column.StickyLeft)
    35	                .AddClass("sticky-right", _column.StickyRight)
    36	                .AddClass($"edit-mode-cell", _dataGrid.EditMode == DataGridEditMode.Cell && _column.Editable)
    37	                .AddClass(_column.CellClassFunc?.Invoke(_item))
    38	                .AddClass(_column.CellClass)
    39	                .Build();
    40	
    41	        internal string ComputedStyle =>
    42	            new StyleBuilder()
    43	                .AddStyle(_column.CellStyleFunc?.Invoke(_item))
    44	                .AddStyle(_column.CellStyle)
    45	                .Build();
    46	
    47	        #endregion
    48	
    49	        public Cell(MudDataGrid<T> dataGrid, Column<T> column, T item)
    50	        {
    51	            _dataGrid = dataGrid;
    52	            _column = column;
    53	            _item = item;
    54	
    55	            OnStartedEditingItem();
    56	
    57	            // Create the CellContext
    58	            _cellContext = new CellContext<T>(_dataGrid, _item);
    59	        }
    60	
    61	        public async Task StringValueChangedAsync(string? value)
    62	        {
    63	            _column.SetProperty(_item, value);
    64	
    65	            // If the edit mode is Cell, we update immediately.
    66	            if (_dataGrid.EditMode == DataGridEditMode.Cell)
    67	                await _dataGrid.CommitItemChangesAsync(_item);
    68	        }
    69	
    70	        public async Task NumberValueChangedAsync(double? value)
    71	        {
    72	            _column.SetProperty(_item, value);
    73	
    74	            // If the edit mode is Cell, we update immediately.
    75	            if (_dataGrid.EditMode == DataGridEditMode.Cell)
    76	                await _dataGrid.CommitItemChangesAsync(_item);
    77	        }
    78	
    79	        private void OnStartedEditingItem()
    80	        {
    81	            if (ComputedValue is null)
    82	            {
    83	                return;
    84	            }
    85	
    86	            if (ComputedValue is JsonElement element)
    87	            {
    88	                if (_column.dataType == typeof(string))
    89	                {
    90	                    _valueString = element.GetString();
    91	                }
    92	                else if (_column.isNumber)
    93	                {
    94	                    _valueNumber = element.GetDouble();
    95	                }
    96	            }
    97	            else
    98	            {
    99	                if (_column.dataType == typeof(string))
   100	                {
   101	                    _valueString = (string)ComputedValue;
   102	                }
   103	                else if (_column.isNumber)
   104	                {
   105	                    _valueNumber = Convert.ToDouble(ComputedValue);
   106	                }
   107	            }
   108	        }
   109	    }
   110	}

  1975	        internal async Task CommitItemChangesAsync(T item)
  1976	        {
  1977	            // Here, we need to validate at the cellular level...
  1978	            if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
  1979	                _ = await CommittedItemChanges(item); // ignore return value in cell edit mode
  1980	
  1981	            await CommittedItemChanged.InvokeAsync(item);
  1982	        }
  1983	
  1984	        /// <summary>
  1985	        /// This method notifies the consumer that changes to the data have been committed
  1986	        /// and what those changes are. This variation of the method is used when the EditMode
  1987	        /// is anything but Cell since the _editingItem is used.
  1988	        /// </summary>
  1989	        /// <returns></returns>
  1990	        internal async Task CommitItemChangesAsync()
  1991	        {
  1992	            Debug.Assert(_editingItem is not null);
  1993	            Debug.Assert(_editForm is not null);
  1994	            await _editForm.ValidateAsync();
  1995	            if (!_editForm.IsValid)
  1996	            {
  1997	                return;
  1998	            }
  1999	
  2000	            if (_editingSourceItem != null)
  2001	            {
  2002	                if (CommittedItemChanges != null)
  2003	                {
  2004	                    var closeBehavior = await CommittedItemChanges(_editingItem);
  2005	
  2006	                    if (closeBehavior == DataGridEditFormAction.KeepOpen)
  2007	                        return;
  2008	                }
  2009	
  2010	                foreach (var property in _properties.Where(p => p.CanWrite))
  2011	                    property.SetValue(_editingSourceItem, property.GetValue(_editingItem));
  2012	
  2013	                await CommittedItemChanged.InvokeAsync(_editingSourceItem);
  2014	
  2015	                ClearEditingItem();
  2016	                _isEditFormOpen = false;
  2017	            }



2026-05-29 12:52:27,117 - INFO - swea-agent-MudBlazor__MudBlazor-12907 - ========================= STEP 59 =========================
2026-05-29 12:52:27,123 - DEBUG - swea-lm-MudBlazor__MudBlazor-12907 - n_cache_control: 2
2026-05-29 12:52:34,346 - DEBUG - swea-lm-MudBlazor__MudBlazor-12907 - Response: ModelResponse(id='35347c696e0648b7a8c4c26d01caaced', created=1780059148, model='DeepSeek-V4-Pro', object='chat.completion', system_fingerprint=None, choices=[Choices(finish_reason='stop', index=0, message=Message(content="The implementation looks correct. Let me summarize what was done:\n\n## Summary of Changes\n\nI made changes to `/testbed/src/MudBlazor/Components/DataGrid/MudDataGrid.razor.cs`:\n\n### 1. Added a new `CommittedItemChanged` event parameter\nAfter the existing `CommittedItemChanges` property, I added a new `EventCallback<T>` parameter called `CommittedItemChanged` with XML documentation that explains it fires after item changes have been committed and applied to the underlying data source.\n\n### 2. Fire `CommittedItemChanged` in cell edit mode (`CommitItemChangesAsync(T item)`)\nAfter `CommittedItemChanges` is called (if not null), `CommittedItemChanged.InvokeAsync(item)` is called with the same item. In cell edit mode, the item is already the source item (modified directly), so this fires after changes are applied.\n\n### 3. Updated `CommitItemChangesAsync(T item)` (cell edit mode - line 1981)\n\nAdded `await CommittedItemChanged.InvokeAsync(item);` after the `CommittedItemChanges` call. In cell edit mode, the item is already the source item with changes applied (since `SetProperty` was called before this method), so `CommittedItemChanged` fires after the commit.\n\n### 3. Updated `CommitItemChangesAsync()` (form edit mode - line 2013)\n\nAdded `await CommittedItemChanged.InvokeAsync(_editingSourceItem);` after the properties are copied from `_editingItem` to `_editingSourceItem` and before `ClearEditingItem()`. This ensures that `CommittedItemChanged` fires only after the changes have been applied to the bound source item, and it passes the actual bound item (not the copy) to the event handler. It also correctly does NOT fire when `CommittedItemChanges` returns `DataGridEditFormAction.KeepOpen`, since in that case changes are not applied.', role='assistant', tool_calls=None, function_call=None, provider_specific_fields={'refusal': None}), provider_specific_fields={'content_filter_results': {}})], usage=Usage(completion_tokens=377, prompt_tokens=30089, total_tokens=30466, completion_tokens_details=None, prompt_tokens_details=None, audio_prompt_tokens=0), service_tier=None, prompt_filter_results=None)
